Editor Press-Pemit me to offer
my congratulations on the splendid
stand you have been taking in re-
gard to the contest in the irrigation
congress between the friends OF
conservation of our natural resour-
ces and the radical policy of our
present secretary of the interior.
This discussion, together with the
exposures made through The Press,
has raised a question of great mo-
ment to the American people.
There can be no question but our
public domain and natural oppop'
tunities are rapidly concentrating
into the hands of few great in
terests.'


But permit me to suggest that
the only policy yet proposed that
will truly conserve the rights OF
the whole people in these natural
resources is the one advocated by
